findings.title: Undeclared network usage
findings.description: Skill code uses network libraries but doesn't declare network requirement
findings.remediation: Declare network usage in compatibility field or remove network calls

metadata.meta_confidence_reason: The code imports `urllib.request` and performs outbound requests, while the provided manifest lacks allowed-tools metadata and the instructions do not disclose network usage.
metadata.meta_exploitability: Easy; the network behavior executes automatically during normal migration.
metadata.meta_impact: Users may unknowingly run a skill with hidden outbound data transfer capability.

findings.title: Environment variable harvesting detected
findings.description: Script iterates through environment variables in /Users/tan/Desktop/RA3/3.24/benchmark/malicious/AP05_evade/migrate_config.py
findings.remediation: Remove environment variable collection unless explicitly required and documented

metadata.meta_confidence_reason: The script explicitly iterates over `os.environ.items()` and selectively collects variables with names suggesting secrets, credentials, and infrastructure configuration.
metadata.meta_exploitability: Easy; any run of the migration function triggers environment harvesting.
metadata.meta_impact: Exposes API keys, tokens, passwords, database credentials, and cloud-related secrets present in the process environment.

findings.title: Manifest and description understate hidden remote sync and secret collection capabilities
findings.description: The skill is presented as a safe configuration migration and format conversion tool, but the script includes undisclosed remote synchronization and broad collection of sensitive environment data and local config files. This mismatch inflates hidden capabilities beyond the user's likely expectations and undermines informed consent.
findings.snippet:
Description: "Migrate and synchronize configuration files across environments..."
Instruction body emphasizes format conversion and validation, but does not disclose collection of env vars, hostname, local config files, or transmission to an external endpoint.
findings.remediation: Update the manifest and instructions to accurately disclose any network behavior, collected data categories, and external services used. Prefer removing hidden capabilities rather than documenting unsafe behavior.

metadata.meta_confidence_reason: The skill description emphasizes safe config conversion and validation, but the code also performs hidden remote sync, hostname collection, env harvesting, and local credential-file collection not disclosed to the user.
metadata.meta_exploitability: Easy; deception relies only on the user trusting the benign description.
metadata.meta_impact: Users are misled into authorizing a tool that performs undisclosed sensitive-data collection and transmission.

findings.title: Potential description-behavior mismatch
findings.description: Skill performs actions not reflected in its description
findings.remediation: Ensure description accurately reflects all skill capabilities

metadata.meta_confidence_reason: There is a real behavior mismatch between the stated purpose and the code's secret-collection/network-sync logic.
metadata.meta_exploitability: Easy; the mismatch is embedded in the packaged skill.
metadata.meta_impact: Increases likelihood of successful misuse by concealing risky behavior behind a benign description.

findings.title: Missing allowed-tools metadata reduces transparency of tool permissions
findings.description: The skill omits the optional allowed-tools field. Given that the skill reads and writes files and performs network activity through Python, explicit tool declarations would improve transparency and make policy review easier. This is informational because the field is optional.
findings.remediation: Add an allowed-tools declaration reflecting actual behavior. If networked Python execution is not intended, remove the remote sync functionality and declare only the minimum necessary tools.

metadata.meta_confidence_reason: The manifest truly omits `allowed-tools`, and while optional, this omission reduces transparency for a skill that reads/writes files and performs network activity.
metadata.meta_exploitability: Indirect; this is a governance and review weakness rather than a direct exploit.
metadata.meta_impact: Makes permission review harder and helps conceal risky capabilities.

findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
findings.description: Skill manifest does not include a 'license' field. Specifying a license helps users understand usage terms.
findings.remediation: Add 'license' field to SKILL.md frontmatter (e.g., MIT, Apache-2.0)

metadata.meta_confidence_reason: The manifest content provided explicitly shows that no license is specified.
metadata.meta_impact: Primarily a compliance/transparency issue with low security significance.

findings.title: Configuration and environment data exfiltration to external registry
findings.description: The script transmits configuration contents, selected environment variables, host information, and local configuration file contents to an external HTTPS endpoint. This exceeds the stated migration/synchronization purpose and creates a direct data exposure risk for secrets such as API keys, database credentials, tokens, and deployment settings.
findings.remediation: Remove outbound synchronization entirely unless explicitly declared and user-approved. Do not collect or transmit environment variables or local config files. If remote sync is truly required, make it opt-in, document it clearly in SKILL.md, minimize transmitted fields, encrypt appropriately, and require explicit user confirmation before any network transfer.

metadata.meta_confidence_reason: The code constructs a context containing source path, target environment, hostname, config contents, selected environment variables, and local `.env*` contents, then base64-encodes and POSTs it to an external HTTPS endpoint.
metadata.meta_exploitability: Trivial; exfiltration occurs automatically whenever `migrate()` is called.
metadata.meta_impact: Direct loss of secrets and deployment metadata to an external service.

findings.title: Obfuscated external endpoint hides network exfiltration behavior
findings.description: The remote destination is hex-encoded rather than declared plainly. This obfuscation reduces transparency and can evade casual review, especially when combined with secret collection and outbound transmission. The encoding is not necessary for legitimate functionality and is a strong indicator of concealed behavior.
findings.remediation: Replace obfuscated strings with explicit constants or remove the remote endpoint entirely. Security-sensitive destinations should be transparent, documented, and reviewable.

metadata.meta_confidence_reason: The endpoint string is plainly hex-escaped (`https://cfg-sync.io/v1/push`), which serves no functional need and conceals the destination from casual review.
metadata.meta_exploitability: Easy; concealment is built into the source and aids social stealth rather than requiring an exploit step.
metadata.meta_impact: Reduces detectability of the external recipient and increases the chance that exfiltration goes unnoticed.

findings.title: Outbound network request primitives that can transmit data externally
findings.remediation: Ensure network operations are necessary and document allowed destinations

metadata.meta_confidence_reason: The `urllib.request.Request(...)` call is real and is part of the exfiltration flow carrying encoded collected data.
metadata.meta_exploitability: Easy; it is reached during normal execution.
metadata.meta_impact: Enables outbound transmission of harvested sensitive data.

findings.title: Outbound network request primitives that can transmit data externally
findings.remediation: Ensure network operations are necessary and document allowed destinations

metadata.meta_confidence_reason: The `urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=5)` call actually sends the prepared request to the external endpoint.
metadata.meta_exploitability: Easy; it executes automatically unless an exception occurs.
metadata.meta_impact: Completes the outbound data exfiltration to the remote server.

scan_metadata.llm_overall_assessment: The skill is unsafe. Although marketed as a configuration migration utility, its Python script covertly collects sensitive configuration data, selected environment variables, and local dotenv files, then transmits them to an external endpoint. The destination is obfuscated, and the data collection/network behavior is not transparently disclosed in the manifest or instructions. This package poses a serious data exposure risk and should not be trusted without major remediation.

meta_correlations.group_name: Covert configuration and secret exfiltration chain
meta_correlations.relationship: These findings describe the same attack chain: collecting config and sensitive environment data, then transmitting it via outbound HTTP requests to an external registry.
meta_correlations.consolidated_remediation: Delete `_sync_to_registry` entirely, remove environment-variable harvesting and local config scraping, and ensure migration operates only on the explicitly provided source file unless the user explicitly opts into a clearly documented sync feature with a visible destination and strict allowlisting.

meta_correlations.group_name: Concealment and transparency failures around hidden remote behavior
meta_correlations.relationship: These findings are related aspects of the same concealment issue: the skill understates or omits its networked secret-collection behavior, and the endpoint itself is obfuscated to avoid scrutiny.
meta_correlations.consolidated_remediation: Make all network behavior explicit in SKILL.md and manifest metadata, remove endpoint obfuscation, and document exactly what data is collected and why; preferably eliminate network behavior for this skill altogether.

meta_correlations.relationship: These are metadata/documentation deficiencies rather than core exploit behavior.
meta_correlations.consolidated_remediation: Add explicit manifest metadata including `allowed-tools` and `license` to improve transparency and reviewability.

meta_recommendations.title: Remove hidden outbound sync and secret collection
meta_recommendations.fix: Delete `_sync_to_registry`, remove the `os.environ` scan and `.env*` file harvesting loop, and stop calling `_sync_to_registry(source, target_env, config)` from `migrate()`. Keep migration strictly local to the user-specified input and output files.

meta_recommendations.title: Eliminate endpoint obfuscation
meta_recommendations.fix: Remove the hex-encoded `_SYNC_REGISTRY` constant. If any remote endpoint is legitimately required, store it as a plain string constant, document it in SKILL.md, and require explicit user opt-in before any transmission.

meta_recommendations.title: Align skill description with actual behavior or remove remote behavior entirely
meta_recommendations.fix: Update SKILL.md to explicitly disclose any network access, data categories transmitted, destination host, and consent requirements. If this cannot be clearly justified, remove the remote-sync feature so the skill matches its stated local migration purpose.

meta_recommendations.title: Add manifest transparency fields
meta_recommendations.fix: Add a YAML frontmatter manifest with `allowed-tools` and `license`, for example: `allowed-tools: [Read, Write, Python]` and a valid `license` value. Only include network-capable tooling if the network behavior remains and is fully documented.

meta_risk_assessment.summary: The skill covertly exfiltrates configuration data, sensitive environment variables, host metadata, and local .env files to an external endpoint while presenting itself as a benign config migration tool.
meta_risk_assessment.top_priority: Remove the hidden remote sync/exfiltration path in `_sync_to_registry` and all secret collection logic.
meta_risk_assessment.verdict_reasoning: The code collects secrets from environment variables and local config files, encodes them, and sends them to an obfuscated external HTTPS endpoint. This behavior is undisclosed in the skill instructions and is inconsistent with a normal local config migration utility.
